entries.title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-05-30
entries.summary: AI agent frameworks and coding agents remain the dominant categories, with revfactory/harness and EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin each gaining hundreds of stars daily, and anthropics/skills continuing its massive sustained run. However, the standout acceleration anomaly is MoneyPrinterTurbo, which added 4,400 stars in 24 hours—a 3x+ jump from its typical velocity—suggesting a surge of interest in AI-driven video production from a single prompt. This cross-source pattern is reinforced by Hacker News top stories focusing on Claude Opus 4.8 and a 'Please Use AI' sentiment, indicating developer curiosity about practical, high-impact AI applications. The TTS category also shows renewed attention with MOSS-TTS's 217-star daily gain, hinting at growing demand for open-source audio generation. If MoneyPrinterTurbo's trajectory continues, it may signal a broader shift from agent frameworks to automated content creation workflows.

entries.title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-05-29
entries.summary: Coding agent / SWE agent projects continue to dominate, with 'Understand-Anything' adding 6,395 stars in 24 hours and 'DeepSeek-Reasonix' accelerating 1.6x to 2,472 daily stars. The category's average daily delta of 3,555 is the highest across all tracked categories. A notable breakout is 'code-review-graph' (2.1x acceleration), which combines MCP tool calling with local code intelligence to achieve 38x–528x token reduction, signaling growing developer interest in cost-efficient, context-aware coding tools. Meanwhile, 'getagentseal/codeburn' (3.2x acceleration) offers a TUI dashboard for tracking AI tool spend, reflecting a parallel concern for observability. The cross-source confirmation of Anthropic's $65B funding and Claude Opus 4.8 release on both Product Hunt and Hacker News suggests enterprise AI momentum may further fuel coding agent adoption. If token-cost optimization tools continue to gain traction, we may see a shift toward leaner agent architectures.

entries.title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-05-28
entries.summary: Coding agent / SWE agent projects continue to command the highest developer attention on GitHub, with four repos in the top five and an average daily delta of 2,007 stars. Understand-Anything added 4,365 stars in 24 hours, while DeepSeek-Reasonix accelerated to 1,599 stars, both showing sustained momentum. The category's dominance is reinforced by cross-source signals: a Hacker News thread titled 'I think Anthropic and OpenAI have found product-market fit' (805 votes) echoes the theme of agentic coding tools gaining traction. Meanwhile, MCP / Tool Calling projects like ECC (1,628 stars) maintain steady interest, but no new category has emerged to challenge coding agents. The sustained focus suggests developers are actively investing in agentic coding workflows, not just experimenting.
